info.name: Playymate — Social & Feed Module
info.description:
End-to-end Postman collection for the Playymate social and feed module.

Covers:
- 📰 Feed (home feed, suggested follows, nearby venues, refresh)
- 📝 Posts (create, read, update, delete, search, location, auto-generated)
- 💬 Comments (create, reply, update, delete, get replies)
- 🖼️ Media (presign upload, confirm)
- 🎬 Reels (presign upload, CRUD, explore, trending, search, view, analytics)
- 📖 Stories (presign upload, CRUD, feed, viewers, highlights)
- ❤️ Likes (like, unlike, toggle, hide/show)
- 🔒 Block & Mute

## Setup
1. Set the `base_url` variable to your server, e.g. `http://localhost:3000`
2. Set `access_token` by running the Login request first (or paste your JWT).
3. All other variables (post_id, comment_id, reel_id, story_id, …) are auto-captured via test scripts.

request.description: Get personalized home feed with venues, events, and friend activity. May include a profile_completion_card for users who haven't completed their profile.
Get suggested users to follow based on interests and connections.
Get nearby venues based on user's location.
Invalidate feed cache to get fresh content on next request.
Create a new post with text, media, visibility settings.

Create a post with image or video. Max 5 media items allowed.

Flow:
1. Use Media > Presign Upload to get upload URL
2. Upload file to S3
3. Use the returned URL in media array
Create a post with location. Use Posts > Get Posts by Location to find posts near a location.
Create a post with multiple media items (image + video) and location. Max 5 media items allowed.
Get a post by ID with author details and engagement counts.
Update a post's content and settings. Only the author can update.
Delete a post. Only the author can delete. Returns undo window info.
Search posts by hashtag.
Get posts within a certain radius of a location.
Get posts by a specific user.
Accept an AI-generated post suggestion.
Decline/remove an AI-generated post suggestion.
Create a comment on a post. Max 300 characters.
Get comments on a post. sort_by: 'recent' | 'popular'
Reply to a comment. Cannot reply to a reply.
Update a comment. Edit window is 5 minutes.
Delete a comment. Author or post owner can delete.
Get replies to a comment.
Create a comment on a reel. Requires a valid {{reel_id}}.
Get comments on a reel. sort_by: 'recent' | 'most_liked'.
Generate presigned URL for uploading media. type: 'image' or 'video'
Confirm media upload after file is uploaded to storage.

Step 1 of upload flow. Returns a presigned PUT URL valid for 5 min.
Client uploads the video directly to Wasabi using upload_url, then passes file_url as video_url when creating the reel.

body.raw:
{
  "video_url": "{{reel_file_url}}",
  "duration": 30,
  "thumbnail_url": "{{reel_thumb_url}}",
  "aspect_ratio": "9:16",
  "title": "Epic Badminton Shot",
  "caption": "Smash of the century! #badminton #sports",
  "hashtags": ["badminton", "sports", "playymate"],
  "mentions": [],
  "visibility": "public",
  "allow_comments": true,
  "allow_duets": false,
  "allow_stitches": false
}

request.description: Pass the file_url returned from presign as video_url.
Fetches reel details and auto-increments view count (first view only from each user).
Returns likes_count, comments_count, views_count, avg_watch_time %, engagement_rate %.
Soft-deletes the reel. Only the owner can do this.
Get reels by a specific user. Returns public reels and follower-only reels if the requesting user follows the target user. Use user_id='me' to get your own reels.

Returns presigned PUT URL (5 min) + 7-day signed GET URL.
purpose: 'story' | 'story_thumbnail'

item.name: 3a. Create Story with Mentions (close friends only)

exec: console.log('story_id with mentions set to:', d.story_id);

request.description: Create a story with mention overlays. Mentions are validated - only followers can be mentioned. Visibility set to close_friends.
Get the authenticated user's story archive (expired stories that were automatically archived). Only visible to owner.
Create a highlight with archived stories. Use story_id from the archive response.

item.name: 4c. Add Archived Story to Existing Highlight

request.description: Add an archived story to an existing highlight. Use original_story_id from archive response.
Get all stories for a specific user (active 24h stories + archived highlights).

item.name: 5. Get Story by ID (marks as viewed)

request.description: Get a single highlight by ID with all stories
Update highlight (owner only)

item.name: H4. Add Story to Highlight (by highlight ID)

request.description: Adds a story to an existing highlight using the highlight_id in the URL.
Delete a highlight (owner only)
Get all stories in a highlight
Track when a user views a highlight
Get list of users who viewed a highlight (owner only)

Share a story to a user's direct message.

Parameters:
- content_id: The story_id to share
- content_type: Must be 'story'
- shared_to: 'direct_message' for DM
- recipient_id: User ID of the recipient
- message: Optional message to attach

Share a story externally (link copy, social media, etc.)

Parameters:
- content_id: The story_id to share
- content_type: Must be 'story'
- shared_to: 'external' for external sharing
- message: Optional message to attach
Get your share history (what content you've shared)

Report a post for violating community guidelines.

target_type: 'post' | 'comment' | 'reel' | 'story' | 'user'
reason: 'spam' | 'harassment' | 'hate_speech' | 'violence' | 'nudity' | 'fake_profile' | 'misinformation' | 'other'
Report a story for violating community guidelines.
Report a reel for violating community guidelines.
Report a comment for violating community guidelines.
Report a user for violating community guidelines (fake profile, etc.)
Get list of reports you've submitted
Withdraw a pending report (can only withdraw reports that are still pending)
Get the authenticated user's close friends list. Returns list of users marked as close friends.
Add a user to close friends by username. User must follow you first. Replace {{target_username}} with the username to add.
Remove a user from close friends by username. Replace {{target_username}} with the username to remove.

exec: console.log('Close friends story_id set to:', d.story_id);

request.description: Create a story visible only to close friends. Only users in your close friends list can see this story.

item.name: 5. Get Story Feed (includes close friends stories)

request.description: Get story feed. Includes stories from followed users and close friends (where viewer is in their close friends list).

content_type: 'reel' | 'post' | 'comment' | 'story'
reaction: 'like' | 'love' | 'haha' | 'wow' | 'sad' | 'angry'
Like a story by story_id

If the user has liked → removes the like, returns liked=false.
If not liked → creates like, returns liked=true.
Block a user. Blocks are bidirectional.
Unblock a user.
Mute a user. Can selectively mute posts, stories, and notifications.
Unmute a user.
Get list of users you have blocked.
Get list of users you have muted.
Check if you have blocked or been blocked by a specific user.
Follow a user.
Unfollow a user.
Get followers list for target user.
Get following list for target user.
Check if current user follows target user and mutual follow state.

item.name: 📋 End-to-End Post Flow
item.description: Run these in order to test the complete post creation → interact → delete lifecycle.

request.description:
Get personalized feed from users you follow.

Query Parameters:
- limit: Number of items to return (default: 20)
- cursor: Pagination cursor for next page

response.name: 200 - Success

request.description: Discover new content from users you don't follow. Personalized recommendations based on your interests.

response.name: 200 - Success

request.description: Get trending content for today. Shows fastest growing content based on engagement velocity.
Get trending content for this week.
Get trending content for this month.
Negative test: Invalid time_period parameter should return 400 error.

response.name: 400 - Invalid time_period
response.body: {"success": false, "error": "INVALID_TIME_PERIOD", "message": "time_period must be one of: today, week, month", "data": null}

request.description: Get performance analytics for your content. Shows impressions, engagement, reach, and insights.

item.name: 8. PUT Update Feed Preferences - Full update

request.description: Update feed preferences with full configuration.

item.name: 9. PUT Update Feed Preferences - Opt out

request.description: Opt out of personalized algorithm.

item.name: 10. PUT Update Feed Preferences - Empty body

request.description: Negative test: Empty body should return 400 error.

response.name: 400 - Empty body error
response.body: {"success": false, "error": "EMPTY_BODY", "message": "Request body must contain at least one preference field", "data": null}

item.name: 11. PUT Update Feed Preferences - Invalid content type

request.description: Negative test: Invalid content type should return 400 error.

response.name: 400 - Validation error
response.body: {"success": false, "error": "VALIDATION_ERROR", "message": "Invalid content types: videos. Must be \"posts\" or \"reels\"", "data": null}

request.description: Clear the social feed cache to force a fresh feed on next request.

response.name: 200 - Success
response.body: {"success": true, "message": "Social feed cache cleared. Next request will generate a fresh feed."}

request.description: Get second page of following feed using cursor from first page response.
Negative test: Request without auth token should return 401 error.
Increment view count on a post. Requires the post_id to be set in variables.
Get creator analytics dashboard. Returns aggregated analytics for authenticated user's content.
Compare multiple content items on a chosen metric (engagement, reach, or views).
Get analytics for a single post. Only the post author can access.
Get analytics for a single reel. Only the reel author can access.
Get analytics for a single story. Only the story author can access.
